meta.title: Silence TTS
meta.subject: A minimal third-party TtsProvider — synthetic WAV silence sized to narration length. The smallest realistic shape a community-built TTS adapter ships in.

beats.narration: This film synthesizes audio through the silence TtsProvider — a community pack. The bytes are real WAV; the duration matches text length; the cascade treats it like any other provider.

scenes.heading: What the silence pack proves
points: Third-party providers register through the same TtsProviderPlugin contract @docent/tts-{openai,elevenlabs,compatible} use.
Capabilities are declared at the plugin level — the engine cross-binds against scene requiresTtsCapabilities.
Provider creation is async and can throw on missing config; the cascade surfaces it before the slow render.

beats.narration: The TtsProvider contract is small. Implement synth, listVoices, capabilities. That's enough.
